name: Chicken and Chili Soup
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup skinless boneless chicken breast, cooked and cubed
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-sized saucepan, melt margarine over medium heat.
2. Add onion, garlic, and 3 tablespoons of broth. Cook, stirring frequently, until the onion is tender (4-5 minutes). If too much liquid evaporates, add a little more broth.
3. Add the remaining broth, cooked chicken, chilies, cauliflower, and kidney beans. Bring the mixture to a boil, cover, and simmer about 10 minutes (until celery is crisp-tender).
4. Meanwhile, in a small bowl or cup, combine cornstarch and cold water.
5. Add this mixture to the broth and cook, stirring until it thickens, about 1 to 2 minutes.
6. Lower the heat so that the soup simmers gently. In two or three batches, add cheese to the soup, stirring well after each batch. Stir until the cheese melts.
7. Continue to stir gently while simmering the soup for an additional 2-3 minutes. Do not allow it to come to a full boil.
8. The soup keeps in the refrigerator for 1-2 days.
